query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Allege', 'To claim without proof', 'Over time, the community learned to allege its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Allegiance', 'Loyalty to a person, group, or cause', 'The author hoped the new policy would allegiance tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Allegory', 'A symbolic narrative', 'The essay highlights the allegory of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alleviate', 'Reduce pain or difficulty', 'Critics argued that the plan might alleviate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alliance', 'The state of being allied, the act of allying or uniting', 'Her alliance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Allocate', 'To distribute resources for a particular purpose', '“The government allocated funds for education.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Allot', 'To distribute by lot', 'Over time, the community learned to allot its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alloy', 'To mix', 'Critics argued that the plan might alloy the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Allude', 'To refer to something indirectly', '“The book alludes to historical events.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Allure', 'The quality of being powerfully attractive', 'The essay highlights the allure of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Allusion', 'An indirect reference, a hint, a reference to something', 'Critics argued that the plan might allusion the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alluvion', 'Overflow, flood', 'The essay highlights the alluvion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ally', 'To unite, or form a connection between', 'He ally overlooked details that later proved decisive.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Almanac', 'A book or table listing nautical, astronomical, etc. Events', 'The essay highlights the almanac of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aloft', 'In or into the air', 'The essay highlights the aloft of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aloof', 'Reserved and remote, physically or emotionally distant', 'Her aloof became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Altar', 'A table or similar flat-topped structure for religious rites', 'The essay highlights the altar of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alter', 'Make a change to', 'Her alter became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alteration', 'The act of making different', 'Scholars noted a striking alteration between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Altercate', 'To argue, quarrel or wrangle with someone', 'Over time, the community learned to altercate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alternate', 'To perform by turns or in succession, interchange regularly', 'Critics argued that the plan might alternate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alternative', 'Relating to a choice between two or more possibilities', 'Over time, the community learned to alternative its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Altitude', 'The height measured from sea level up to any given point', 'Over time, the community learned to altitude its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Alto', 'A musical section higher than tenor and lower than soprano', 'Her alto became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Altruism', 'Selfless concern for the well-being of others', 'Scholars noted a striking altruism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Altruistic', 'Showing selfless concern for others', 'What seemed altruistic at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amalgam', 'A combination of different things', 'Scholars noted a striking amalgam between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amalgamate', 'To combine or unite to form one organization', 'The author hoped the new policy would amalgamate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amass', 'To gather together or accumulate', 'The author hoped the new policy would amass tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amateur', 'Non-professional', 'Her amateur became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amatory', 'Of or relating to love', 'The author hoped the new policy would amatory tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambiance', 'Mood, quality, atmosphere', 'The essay highlights the ambiance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambidextrous', 'Able to use both hands equally well; versatile', 'The author hoped the new policy would ambidextrous tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambiguity', 'Uncertainty or lack of clarity', '“The ambiguity of the statement caused confusion.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambiguous', 'Open to multiple interpretations', '“Her response was ambiguous and left room for doubt.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambitious', 'Possessing, or controlled by ambition', 'The passage presents a ambitious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambivalence', 'Mixed feelings or contradictory attitudes', '“His ambivalence about moving was evident.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambivalent', 'Having mixed feelings about someone or something; being unsure', 'What seemed ambivalent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amble', 'Slow walk, stroll', 'The essay highlights the amble of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambrosial', 'Succulently sweet or fragrant', 'The essay highlights the ambrosial of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambulance', 'Emergency vehicle that transports sick or injured people', 'Scholars noted a striking ambulance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambulate', 'To relocate one''s self under the power of one''s own legs', 'Critics argued that the plan might ambulate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ambush', 'To act of lying in concealment as to attack by surprise', 'The author hoped the new policy would ambush tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ameliorate', 'To make something better or improve a situation', 'Over time, the community learned to ameliorate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amenable', 'Open and responsive to suggestion', 'Critics argued that the plan might amenable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amend', 'To make better, to change', 'Critics argued that the plan might amend the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amenity', 'Pleasantness', 'The essay highlights the amenity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Americanism', 'A trait particuliar to the us or its citizens', 'Critics argued that the plan might americanism the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amiable', 'Having a friendly manner', 'The passage presents a amiable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Amicable', 'Characterized by friendliness', 'What seemed amicable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
